theme.title: Direct and Indirect Objects
theme.description: Practice direct object pronouns, indirect object pronouns, and combinations of both in practical Spanish.

lesson.title: Lesson 3: Direct and Indirect Together
lesson.instructions: Combine indirect and direct object pronouns in the same sentence with useful everyday verbs.

exercises.prompt: I give the book to Maria. I give it to her.
exercises.answer: Le doy el libro a Maria. Se lo doy.
I am sending it to her tonight.
exercises.answer_alt: I send it to her tonight.
exercises.explanation: 'Se la' means 'to her' plus a feminine singular direct object.

exercises.prompt: Can you explain the problem to me? Can you explain it to me?
exercises.answer: Puedes explicarme el problema? Puedes explicarmelo?
exercises.answer_alt: Me puedes explicar el problema? Me lo puedes explicar?
exercises.explanation: The second sentence needs both an indirect and a direct object pronoun.

exercises.answer: I do not want to sell them to him.
exercises.answer_alt: I do not want to sell them to her.
exercises.explanation: 'Se' replaces 'le', and 'los' is the direct object.

exercises.prompt: We are going to show the photos to our parents. We are going to show them to them.
exercises.answer: Vamos a mostrar las fotos a nuestros padres. Se las vamos a mostrar.
exercises.answer_alt: Vamos a mostrar las fotos a nuestros padres. Vamos a mostrarselas.
exercises.explanation: Use 'se las' for 'to them' plus a feminine plural object.

exercises.prompt: Siempre se lo digo a mis estudiantes.
exercises.answer: I always tell it to my students.
exercises.explanation: Natural English often uses 'that' instead of repeating the direct object literally.

exercises.prompt: She is reading the email to me because I cannot see it well.
exercises.answer: Ella me esta leyendo el correo porque no puedo verlo bien.
exercises.answer_alt: Ella esta leyendome el correo porque no puedo verlo bien.
exercises.explanation: This sentence includes an indirect object with one verb and a direct object pronoun with another.

exercises.prompt: Te lo voy a devolver manana.
exercises.answer: I am going to give it back to you tomorrow.
exercises.explanation: 'Te lo' combines indirect and direct object pronouns.
